symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical bilateral swelling and pain of both wrists, MCP and PIP joints

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical small-joint (wrist/MCP/PIP) synovitis is the hallmark distribution of rheumatoid-pattern inflammatory arthritis.
Septic arthritis is characteristically monoarticular; a bilaterally symmetrical polyarticular pattern makes joint sepsis much less likely, though not impossible.
Prominent MCP involvement with soft swelling is not typical of osteoarthritis, which spares the MCPs and gives hard bony nodes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged early-morning stiffness lasting more than three hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Morning stiffness lasting more than an hour (here >3h) is a defining feature of inflammatory, as opposed to mechanical, arthritis.
Osteoarthritis produces brief (<30 minute) gelling stiffness, not prolonged inflammatory stiffness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Flare onset within 48 hours of running out of the regular anti-inflammatory (meloxicam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear medication-withdrawal precipitant strongly favours a rebound inflammatory flare of a known condition over a new infective process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known background inflammatory arthritis under investigation, with previous similar flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An established chronic inflammatory-arthritis context with recurrent flares makes a further flare the leading explanation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound fatigue with subjective (unmeasured) low-grade feverishness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional fatigue and low-grade systemic upset accompany active inflammatory arthritis.
Subjective feverishness is non-specific; a measured high fever with rigors would raise infection, but that is absent here (the red-herring feature).

symptoms.symptom_name: No single disproportionately hot bright-red joint and no tracking erythema up the forearm (patient/video observable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a single hot bright-red joint and of tracking erythema reduces the likelihood of joint sepsis and cellulitis — but a video/self-report negative cannot fully exclude infection, so it must not stand down the safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical soft spongy synovitis with warmth, tender and painful on active/passive movement, no erythema, normal capillary refill (in-person palpation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Soft spongy synovitis without erythema is the hallmark of inflammatory synovitis — a sealed clinician palpation the telehealth AI cannot obtain.
Symmetrical non-erythematous synovitis with intact capillary refill argues against a tracking soft-tissue infection — but only a clinician present can assess it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals essentially normal and afebrile (Temp 37.4, HR 92, BP 125/80, RR 16, SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Near-normal observations with no true fever lower the likelihood of systemic sepsis; no home device was reported, so these are sealed clinician measurements, not patient-obtainable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inflammatory markers (CRP/ESR), RF/anti-CCP serology, hand radiographs, and joint aspiration with synovial-fluid microscopy and culture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Joint aspiration with microscopy and culture is the definitive test to exclude septic arthritis; never available in real time via telehealth.
Negative RF/anti-CCP with raised inflammatory markers and an erosive/inflammatory pattern supports the seronegative rheumatoid diagnosis on formal workup.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of symmetrical small-joint synovitis, prolonged morning stiffness, a known inflammatory-arthritis context and a clear NSAID-cessation trigger is far more consistent with a flare than with joint sepsis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR) and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline markers of inflammatory activity and a screen for the systemic response that would accompany infection — arranged through the prompt GP review.
Confirms the seronegative pattern and supports the rheumatology referral and DMARD assessment.
Assesses for erosive change and provides a baseline for disease monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration with synovial-fluid microscopy, crystal examination and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reserved for any joint that becomes disproportionately hot/red — the definitive test to exclude septic arthritis; not required for a symmetrical flare with no single hot joint.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency-department septic screen with blood cultures and empirical IV antibiotics
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Over-investigation/over-treatment of a benign symmetrical polyarticular flare with a negative red-flag screen; reserve for a genuine hot single joint or systemic sepsis, not routinely.
